summary: Hotfix verification PASSED. Backend regression (test_proptech_backend.py + test_task2_model_migration.py + test_task4_audit_logs.py) = 54/54 PASS. Frontend e2e verified on live preview origin https://placeholder-dash.preview.emergentagent.com: (1) Login admin@placeholder.in/admin123 -> 200 + navigates to /app. (2) /app/memo renders cleanly with memo-page, memo-form, memo-history testids present; NO error-boundary-fallback in DOM. (3) GET /api/memos returns 200 with array; history container shows existing 16 memo rows on first load. (4) Form submission (City=Bengaluru, size=1200, verified_only=false) -> POST /api/valuation-memo returns 200 with Content-Type: application/pdf. (5) After generation, loadHistory re-fires; counter increments from 16 to 17 ('MY MEMOS (17)'); the new entry appears at data-testid=memo-history-0 with text 'Bengaluru · Apartment · 1200 sqft · 12 comps · ₹1.98 Cr'. (6) Sibling pages /app/developments, /app/comps, /app/calculator, /app/data-sources all render WITHOUT the ErrorBoundary fallback. Hotfix is solid.

issues: React console warning: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop' inside Calculator render method.

React hydration-style warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' — an icon/span is being rendered inside a native <option> element which is invalid HTML. Both are non-fatal (page renders, no ErrorBoundary fallback) but should be cleaned up.

action_items: OPTIONAL/minor: Clean up Calculator.jsx — remove <span> children of <option> (use plain text in <option>) and add stable key props to its list render. Not blocking.
Carry-forward from iteration_3: Task 3 global-60/min 429 JSON shape inconsistency remains open (LIMITER_ENABLED=false in /app/backend/.env suppresses it in test mode). Re-evaluate before production.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/pages/ValuationMemo.js: history state + loadHistory + useEffect correctly added at lines 15-30. Defensive Array.isArray check on /api/memos response prevents the original 'history.map is not a function' crash. loadHistory is re-fired inside generate().then which keeps the list fresh after each memo. Verified empirically: counter went 16 -> 17, no duplicate keys, no stale state.

/app/backend/.env CORS_ORIGINS: now explicit comma-separated list including the live preview origin + the alt 76783c70 origin + http://localhost:3000 — correct for credentialed (withCredentials:true) axios calls. No wildcard. Frontend and backend served from same placeholder-dash origin so cookies flow cleanly (confirmed: /api/auth/login set Set-Cookie; subsequent /api/memos and /api/valuation-memo carried session and returned 200).
ErrorBoundary remains a transparent pass-through — no fallback rendered on memo or any sibling page during this run.
updated_files: /app/test_reports/pytest/iteration5_regression.xml (new — junit output of the 54-test regression run)
test_credentials: admin@placeholder.in / admin123 — works (POST /api/auth/login returned 200, cookies set, /api/me + /api/memos + /api/valuation-memo all authenticated correctly)
seed_data_creation: Test generated 1 additional valuation memo doc in the memos collection (Bengaluru / Apartment / 1200 sqft / All sources / ₹1.98 Cr). Not cleaned up — harmless test artifact in admin user's history. Prior 16 memos already existed from earlier iterations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Always use frontend host https://placeholder-dash.preview.emergentagent.com (same origin as REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, so axios withCredentials:true works). Do NOT use the 76783c70 alias — cookies get stripped by Cloudflare's ACAO rewrite. For backend curl/pytest, hit https://placeholder-dash.preview.emergentagent.com/api/* directly. Note: the dashboard's /api/health endpoint returns 404 — there's no health route registered; use /api/me with cookies or /api/markets as a liveness check instead. LIMITER_ENABLED=false in backend/.env (intentional for test convenience).
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause of demo crash was missing useState/useEffect in /app/frontend/src/pages/ValuationMemo.js — `history` was referenced in JSX as `history.map(...)` but the window's global `history` object (a History DOM interface, not an array) was being resolved instead, hence 'history.map is not a function'. Fix added local state `const [history, setHistory] = useState([])` + `loadHistory()` async fn (with Array.isArray defensive check) + `useEffect(() => loadHistory(), [])` to hydrate on mount and a second loadHistory() after generate() — all verified working end-to-end. Secondary fix: CORS_ORIGINS swapped from '*' to explicit origin list to support credentialed axios requests.
